Aesc..Back: The back is the seat of much trouble, especially low down in the back, through the sacrum and hips; although there is also aching all along the back and pain in the back of the neck.
Aesc..Back, pain: Constant dull backache; walking is almost impossible; scarcely able to rise or walk after sitting. You will see one suffering from the Aesculus backache, on attempting to rise from sitting, make many painful efforts before he finally succeeds.
Aesc..Extremities: A peculiar kind of plethora is found running through this remedy, a vascular fullness which affects the extremities and the whole body. Fullness of the hands and feet, not the fullness that pits upon pressure, that we call oedema, but a tenseness.
Aesc..Generalities: It is a general venous stasis, and is sometimes worse in sleep, worse from lying, better from bodily exertion. The symptoms pass away after considerable exertion; moving about, doing something, keeping busy relieves.
Aesc..Rectum: We find in the text over two pages devoted to the symptoms of the rectum. Great soreness; much pain; urging to stool; dark stool followed by white one, showing the liver engorgement.
Aesc..Rectum, constipation: Digestion is slow, the bowels are constipated and there is protrusion of the rectum when at stool. Chronic constipation.
Aesc..Rectum, pain: Sticking, jagging, burning pains, as if the rectum were full of sticks. Great suffering with blind hemorrhoids.
Aesc..Rectum, pain, burning: Sticking, jagging, burning pains, as if the rectum were full of sticks.
Aesc..Stomach: Constant distress and burning in the stomach. Inclination to vomit. It has a great disturbance of digestion. As soon as the patient has swallowed the food, or a little while after, it becomes sour and he eructates it, until after a while he has emptied the stomach of its contents.
Aesc..Abdomen, pain: After eating there is distress in the bowels and rectum. Sticking, jagging, burning pains.
Aesc..Clinical: It is one of the most frequently indicated remedies in the hemorrhoidal constitution, as it used to be called.
Aesc..Abdomen, liver: The liver is full of suffering. If we read the symptoms of the right hypochondrium, of the abdomen and of the rectum, we shall see from the study of these that there must be a marked portal stasis.
Aesc..Back, sacrum: This pain through the sacrum and into the hips, when walking, is a striking feature of Aesculus, so striking that you may expect it to be present even when there are no hemorrhoids.
Aesc..Abdomen: The abdomen is full of troubles. If we read the symptoms of the right hypochondrium, of the abdomen and of the rectum, we shall see from the study of these that there must be a marked portal stasis.
Aesc..Throat: This remedy produces inflammation of the throat, the characteristic being that it is very dark. It has the tendency to produce varicose veins and ulceration, and round about these we have marked duskiness.
Aesc..Back, pain, sacral region: This pain through the sacrum and into the hips, when walking, is a striking feature of Aesculus, so striking that you may expect it to be present even when there are no hemorrhoids. Constant dull backache; walking is almost impossible; scarcely able to rise or walk after sitting.
Aesc..Rectum, hemorrhoids: Great suffering with blind hemorrhoids. The hemorrhoidal veins are all distended and ulcerate. The stool becomes jammed into the rectum, against these distended veins, and then ulceration takes place with bleeding and great suffering. This remedy is often supposed to be suitable to hemorrhoids that do not bleed, but it cures bleeding piles also.
Aesc..Abdomen, pain, liver: The liver is full of suffering. Most troublesome hemorrhoids with fullness of the right hypochondrium.
Aesc..Back, pain, aching, dull pain: Constant dull backache; walking is almost impossible; scarcely able to rise or walk after sitting. You will see one suffering from the Aesculus backache, on attempting to rise from sitting, make many painful efforts before he finally succeeds.
